utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both agents completed successfully and produced output files.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is more effective overall. Both agents performed strongly across most findings (J1–J5, J7), with both correctly identifying PHI in URLs, minimum necessary violations, the missing BAA as a blocker, localStorage exposure, audit logging gaps, and JWT scope deficiencies. The decisive differentiator is J6 (clinical decision safety escalation): the wi_skills agent explicitly flagged that AI-generated discharge summaries influencing clinical decisions require a separate clinical/patient-safety review beyond HIPAA compliance—a HIPAA-skill-specific guardrail. The wo_skills agent treated the LLM feature solely as a privacy/BAA issue and did not escalate it as a clinical safety concern. The wi_skills output also tended to be more precisely structured with HIPAA-specific framing (e.g., minimum necessary principle applied granularly, explicit BAA blocking language), while the wo_skills output—though comprehensive—lacked the clinical safety escalation dimension that separates a HIPAA-specialized review from a general security review.
